author: Diana Mercado, LCSW
content:
SOCIAL WORK CONTACT NOTE — 6/18/2026, 3:00 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Contact type: In-person at Esperanza Health (FQHC)
Interpreter: In-person Spanish-speaking medical assistant (Ms. Lopez), additional family present (mother and aunt).

REASON FOR CONTACT:
Follow-up on patient's Medicaid application status (submitted 2/20/2026 with my assistance) and prescription-assistance program enrollments. Patient was advised by rheumatology (Dr. Tanaka, 4/10/2026) that ongoing specialty follow-up is essential and concerning loss-to-follow-up was anticipated given coverage barriers.

INTERVAL UPDATE:
- Maryland Medicaid application: DENIED on 5/29/2026. Reason cited: 'documentation of immigration status and verification of income from non-traditional employment incomplete.' Specifically the patient's housekeeping work is paid in cash with no W-2 or 1099, and the redetermination process specifically requires documentation. The denial letter (in English) was received by mother who did not understand the appeal timeline. The 90-day appeal window expires 8/27/2026.
- Hopkins financial-aid one-time visit grant: USED for 4/10/2026 rheumatology visit. Per program rules, additional visits require reapplication and are not guaranteed.
- HCQ 90-day patient-assistance supply (from rheumatology visit): supply runs out approximately 7/10/2026. We initiated a renewal request to the manufacturer's PAP today; expected processing 2-3 weeks.
- Prednisone taper: Completed approximately 6/1/2026 per rheum plan. Patient self-reports tolerated well. Joint symptoms returned to pre-prednisone baseline within 7-10 days of completion.
- Levothyroxine: Patient transitioned to $4 generic pricing at Walgreens; affordable.
- Esperanza Health PCP visit: Last seen 4/24/2026; next scheduled 8/2/2026.
- Ophthalmology referral: Still not completed. Patient cannot afford even the sliding-scale visit currently.

PATIENT STATUS / FAMILY REPORT:
Ms. Sandoval reports that her joint symptoms have worsened in the 2 weeks since prednisone taper completion. She is once again struggling with morning stiffness >60 minutes and grip difficulty at work; she has reduced her cleaning shifts from 25-30 hours/week to ~20 hours due to fatigue and joint pain. She is concerned that if her HCQ supply lapses, she will get worse before she sees rheumatology again. Mother is anxious about the Medicaid denial and the financial burden.

Food security: Family reports they are now 'stretching' food more than they used to. Patient reports skipping breakfast 4-5 days per week to save money. Mother brings home leftovers from her domestic-work job when possible. Family is enrolled in Maryland Food Bank distribution (monthly).

Mental health: Patient acknowledges feeling overwhelmed and sad about her health and the family's financial situation. Denies SI/HI. Has not previously seen a mental health provider. Spanish-speaking community mental health resources offered.

INTERVENTIONS TODAY:
1. Filed Medicaid denial APPEAL with patient and mother — paperwork completed in Spanish, supporting documentation gathered (paystubs from informal employment over past 6 months, lease document, utility bill in mother's name showing residency, immigration documents). Appeal hearing scheduled for 8/12/2026.
2. Submitted manufacturer PAP renewal application for hydroxychloroquine (Plaquenil — sanofi-aventis program).
3. Submitted PAP application for levothyroxine (backup if generic pricing changes).
4. Provided list of free/low-cost Spanish-speaking mental health resources (Esperanza Behavioral Health intake number, Casa de Maryland counseling line).
5. Provided WIC re-enrollment information (although patient does not have young children, the food-bank pathway is reinforced).
6. Reviewed return-precautions and signs of worsening connective tissue disease with patient and mother again, in Spanish.
7. Coordinated with rheumatology administrative staff at Hopkins regarding financial-aid renewal pathway for next scheduled visit (7/10/2026).

ASSESSMENT:
High-risk patient with active autoimmune disease and severely fragmented healthcare access. The Medicaid denial is a major barrier and is the proximate threat to maintenance of her HCQ supply and rheumatology follow-up. Mental-health concerns are emerging in the context of chronic illness and social stress.

PLAN:
- Medicaid appeal hearing prep: I will meet with patient and mother again on 7/30/2026 to prepare.
- Monitor PAP application status; intervene if not approved by 7/7/2026.
- Re-engage at next PCP visit (8/2/2026) and after rheumatology if appointment is funded.
- Patient may walk in to Esperanza social work for urgent needs.

Contact time: 75 minutes (including interpreter and document review).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Diana Mercado, LCSW
Esperanza Health Social Work
Date: 6/18/2026
